titleStmt.title: SII 31.80: original edition by C. A. Padmanabha Sastri and T. S. Ravishankar
No. 80. (A.R. No. B 254 of 1938.) PĀCHIKALAPĀḌU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.

summary.p: Slab set up in front of the Chakradharēśvaraśvāmi temple.
It is dated in Śaka 1458 Durmukhi, Mārgaśīra, śu. 12, [Monday] corresponding to November 24, 1536 A.D. The day is Friday not Monday.

Records the gift of the village Pāsugulapāḍu situated in the Ghaṇḍikōṭa-sīma to the God Śrī Tiruvēngaḷanāthadēva by Penuṁgoṇḍa Virīppaṇam. The gift of the village Pāsugulapāḍu is lying in between the villages Chaḍupurāla on northern side, Pamdiḷḷapaḷḷi on north-eastern side, Yaraguḍiapāḍu on east, Kōrappona on eastern side, Gaḍḍamayya palli in south, Kāmalapuram on north-eastern side.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . śrījayābhyudaya śālivāhana śakavarṣaṁbulu 1
458 Agunēṭi durmakhi saṁvatsara mārgaśira śu 12 śupu
ṇyakālāna śrīmanmahādēvadēvōttama śrītiruveṁgaḷanāṁ
dhadēvuniki śrīmanmahārājādhirāja rājaparamēsva(śva)ra śrīvīrapratā
pa Acyutadēvarāya mahārāyaluṁgāriki puṇyaṁgānu pe
nuṁgoṁḍda vīrappaṇaṁ gāru gaṁḍdikōṭa śī(si)malōnu cadupure(rā)laku
vuttarāna paṁdiḷa(ḷla)palliki yīśānyana yaraguḍipāṭiki tūrpunanu kō
ṭhapōnaku Āgnēyananu saṁbaṭūriki dakṣiṇānanu gaḍḍamayyapalleku
nayi(nai)ritini kāmulāpurānaku paḍumara madhyadēśa mayinaṁ
pāsugulapāḍu sahiraṇyōdaka dāna dhārāpūrvakamuṁgānu dhāra
vōsi samarpistēni yī pāsugulapāḍuki nidhinikṣēpaṇajala
pāṣāṇa Ākṣiṇa Āsāmiśiddhasādyu lani Aṣṭabhōgatēja sāmya
savōtpatti samastāvaḷi sahitamuṁgānu pāsugulapāḍu tri
vācānu dhāravōsi tiruveṁgaḷanāṁdha dēvunikīyistimi . yīdhaṁ
rmmaṁ yavvaruṁganaga sēspinānu Asva(śva)mēdhayāgaṁ śēśina phalaṁga
ddu yiṁduku yavvaru ganaka tappinānu vāraṇāsi lōnu gōha
tyabraṁhatya pātakānaṁbōvuvāru . svadattādviguṇaṁ puṇya
paradattānu pālanaṁ paradattāṁ pahārēṇa sa(sva)dattāṁ niṣpalaṁ
bhavēt . maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī śrī jēyunnuṁ ...
Digital edition of SII 31.80 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
